title: alpinia pinetorum
intro: Alpinia pinetorum, also known as the mountain ginger or pine ginger, is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and Southeast Asia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and inflammation. The plant has been used to alleviate nausea, relieve pain, and reduce fever, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Alpinia pinetorum is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
